rag_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope
Place implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the component type or implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL structure when modifying or adding annex blocks
Ensure that all identifiers used in AGREE annexes are visible in the current scope
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope based on the component type or implementation context
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
For input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees, use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
